description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a flat top surface featuring evenly spaced, parallel grooves running across its width. The grooves are deep and create a striped pattern, alternating between raised stone and recessed channels. The block is solid and has a uniform, dark coloration with a slightly mottled stone texture visible on the top surface. The sides of the block are smooth and unadorned, contrasting with the textured top. The overall appearance suggests durability and heaviness, suitable for use as a barrier or obstacle in urban environments. The design is utilitarian, likely intended to prevent passage or demarcate space while allowing water drainage through the grooves.
description: A solid rectangular stone block with deep, parallel grooves on its top surface, creating a striped pattern. The sides are smooth and the block appears heavy and durable.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running horizontally across the top.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth and spacing of the grooves, with the stripes appearing more pronounced.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the same horizontal grooves and smooth sides.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the deep, evenly spaced grooves and the block's thickness.
attribute_location_context: urban sidewalk or plaza as a barrier or seating element
attribute_installation_method: set in concrete or on a prepared gravel base
attribute_weathering_pattern: No visible weathering; the surface appears clean and well-maintained with no chips or discoloration.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: isolated or aligned in a row with similar blocks
